body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia (BNI) has relocated its Singapore overseas office to BNI Tower at 30 Raffles Place, expanding its operations to two floors and offering a range of financial services, including remittance, account management, and financing services. The relocation aims to improve service accessibility for international customers, with BNI's overseas offices, including Singapore, contributing 10% to the company's total assets, with credit syndication and trade finance being the largest contributors.
title: BNI relocates Singapore office to Raffles Place to enhance service accessibility for international customers.

body: PT Merdeka Gold Copper's revenue soared 110.19% year-on-year to USD 1.09 billion in H1 2024, driven by strong domestic and export sales, with significant contributions from key clients. The company's gross profit grew 9.23% to USD 88.70 billion, operating profit jumped 23.71% to USD 62.17 billion, and net profit reached USD 20.53 million, a significant turnaround from a loss of USD 54.91 million in H1 2023.
title: Merdeka Gold's Revenue Soars 110% in First Half 2024, Company Returns to Profitability

body: Individual investors and companies made significant transactions in the Indonesian market, with Iwa Sukresno Karunia increasing his stake in PT Arkadia Digital Media TBK to 25.24%, while others such as Low Tuck Kwong, Eko Desriyanto, and DBS Bank Ltd acquired shares in various companies including PT Bayan Resources TBK, PT Idea Indonesia TBK, and PT Lippo Karawaci TBK. Meanwhile, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama and PT Tiga Makin Jaya sold significant shares in PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera TBK and PT Panca Mitra Multiperdana TBK, respectively, among other notable transactions.
title: Several investors and companies engage in share transactions in various Indonesian companies across different industries.

body: On Wednesday, October 2, foreign investors bought a net 712.63 million shares, with top purchases including PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK (808.16 million shares), PT Bumi Resources Minerals TBK (217.14 million shares), and PT Medco Energi Internasional TBK (76.52 million shares). Meanwhile, foreign investors sold a significant number of shares in companies such as PT Darma Henwa TBK (-153.25 million shares), PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur TBK (-102.98 million shares), and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia TBK (-87.55 million shares).
title: Foreign investors buy 6.15 billion shares, led by digital company PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, with a net surplus of 712.63 million shares.

body: Low Tuck Kwong, founder of PT Bayan Resources, increased his stake to 40.16% by acquiring over 1.5 billion shares, while other investors made notable purchases, including Agus Sanusi's 5.12% stake in PT Lupromax Pelumas Indonesia and PT Arthakencana Rayatama's acquisition of 35.17 million shares in PT Akr Corporindo. Meanwhile, several investors, including DBS Bank and Ruhong Holding, adjusted their stakes in various companies, such as PT Lippo Karawaci, PT Petrosea, and PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera.
title: Multiple investors and companies acquire shares in various Indonesian firms, including coal mining, lubricants, and property development.

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia (BNI) is set to launch BNI Direct, a digital platform that integrates various services for corporate users, including issuing letters of credit, bank guarantees, and cash management, this month. The platform aims to facilitate current corporate users, following the successful launch of BNI's superapp, Wondr BNI, which has been utilized by 3.4 million users.
title: BNI to Launch Digital Platform BNI Direct to Enhance Services for Corporate Users

body: The Financial Services Authority is investigating two Barito Group companies, PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk and PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi Tbk, over allegations of false trading, with shares skyrocketing 794.23% and 3,286% respectively since their IPOs. The companies' share structures show controlling shareholders owning 64.66% and 85.07% of shares, with free float ratios of 11.66% and 14.91%, respectively.
title: Financial Services Authority Investigates Two Barito Group Companies Over Alleged False Trading in Stock Market.
